name: DeepSeek-V3-0324
archDisplay: DeepSeekMoE: 256 routed + 1 shared expert, top-8 routing, 61 layers (3 dense + 58 MoE), Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA)
note: March 2025 refresh of V3 with big reasoning/math/coding/front-end gains (AIME 39.6 to 59.4) and improved function calling and JSON output; MIT-licensed.
tpsNote: 671B MoE far exceeds a single 24GB 4090; typically deployed on multi-GPU servers or via CPU-offload dynamic GGUF quants (~150-250GB range).

results.notes: AIME 2024, up from 39.6 in Dec-2024 V3

MATH-500 pass@1 score (94.0, up from 90.2 for DeepSeek-V3), read directly from the bar chart image (figures/0324_comparison.png) embedded in the official HF model card — this fifth benchmark isn't in the card's text callouts (only MMLU-Pro/GPQA/AIME/LiveCodeBench are called out in prose) but is clearly labeled in the chart itself, which also matches the 4 already-recorded scores exactly (MMLU-Pro 81.2, GPQA Diamond 68.4, AIME 2024 59.4, LiveCodeBench 49.2).

Aider polyglot coding benchmark (225 exercises across 6 languages), diff edit format, run against the DeepSeek API as 'deepseek-chat' (which pointed to V3-0324 at test time); percent-correct (pass-rate-2) 55.1%, 99.6% well-formed. Independent third-party measurement, not vendor self-reported.

SWE-bench Verified resolved rate for DeepSeek V3-0324 run with the OpenHands agent scaffold (not DeepSeek's own harness), published by the OpenHands/All Hands AI team in their 'Introducing OpenHands LM 32B' post as the best open-weights model result at the time. A harder, agentic coding eval distinct from the vendor's own non-agentic LiveCodeBench/MATH-500 numbers already on file.

Live LMArena (Chatbot Arena) text Elo, read from the current (mid-2026) leaderboard snapshot: 1396±4, rank ~130 of tracked models, 45,480 votes. Elo is dynamic and decays in relative rank as newer frontier models are added — this is the model's current standing, not a fixed launch-week score. (DeepSeek's own announcement threads separately claimed a #5 rank / #1-open-model position on the arena shortly after the March 2025 launch, and a #4 WebDev Arena ranking, but did not publish a specific Elo number, so those claims aren't independently reproducible as a score here.)

Humanity's Last Exam, Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1, row labeled 'DeepSeek V3 0324' (5%, vs. 4% for the separately-tracked Dec-2024 'DeepSeek V3'). A hard, unfavorable result included for honest coverage — near the bottom of the tracked comparison set.
IFBench (instruction-following), Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1, row labeled 'DeepSeek V3 0324' (41%, vs. 35% for the separately-tracked Dec-2024 'DeepSeek V3').

Terminal-Bench v2.1 (agentic coding/terminal-use eval), Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1, row labeled 'DeepSeek V3 0324' at 14% — mapped to the 'terminal-bench-2' taxonomy slug for the v2 benchmark family. A hard agentic eval where this non-agentic-era model scores near the bottom of the comparison set (vs. 17% for the Dec-2024 DeepSeek-V3, both far behind current frontier models at 78-88%), included for honest, non-cherry-picked coverage.

GDPval-AA v2 (Artificial Analysis's Elo-based adaptation of OpenAI's GDPval agentic real-world-work benchmark), Intelligence Index v4.1, row labeled 'DeepSeek V3 0324' scored at the floor (0%), tied with Llama-4-Maverick and the Dec-2024 DeepSeek-V3. Reported as-is: a genuinely weak, unfavorable result on a hard agentic benchmark that postdates this model's design, included for honest coverage rather than omitted.

Independent re-measurement of GPQA Diamond by Artificial Analysis (Intelligence Index v4.1 methodology), row explicitly labeled 'DeepSeek V3 0324' (distinct from their separately-tracked 'DeepSeek V3 (Dec)' row, which scored 56%). This 65% is lower than DeepSeek's own self-reported 68.4 already on file — a genuine self-reported vs. independently-verified discrepancy worth surfacing, not a duplicate. Exact evaluation date not published by Artificial Analysis; page reflects their current, periodically-rerun index.
Princeton HAL 'HAL Generalist Agent' scaffold, DeepSeek V3 (March 2025 snapshot = the V3-0324 update, distinct from original Dec-2024 V3). GAIA validation set.

verificationNotes: Existence and release confirmed via DeepSeek's own news post (api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news250325, published 2025-03-25 referencing a 3/24 model card date) and the live HF card, both stating MIT license. Note: HF safetensors index sums to ~685B raw tensor weight but DeepSeek's own papers/cards and consistent third-party reporting describe the architecture as 671B total/37B active (685B includes the multi-token-prediction head), so 671B is kept as the standard-cited figure. All 4 result rows (AIME 59.4, GPQA 68.4, MMLU-Pro 81.2, LiveCodeBench 49.2) confirmed literally on the HF card as the 'after' values of the delta callouts. Caveat (not changed in record): DeepSeek's own 'deepseek-chat' API alias now silently routes to a newer V4-Flash model (since ~Apr 2026) and deprecates 2026-07-24, so the $0.27/$1.10 figure is the original 2025 DeepSeek-hosted launch price, not what DeepSeek's API bills today; the open weights remain genuinely available via self-host or third-party providers.
